name: Persian Magic
description: Persian Magic, deeply rooted in ancient Iranian traditions, encompasses a diverse range of practices aimed at influencing the world through supernatural means. Its origins can be traced back to Zoroastrianism and pre-Zoroastrian beliefs, incorporating elements of astrology, numerology, and herbalism. A key concept is the battle between good (Ahura Mazda) and evil (Angra Mainyu), with magic often employed to bolster the forces of light and ward off darkness. Practitioners utilize incantations, amulets, talismans, and rituals to achieve desired outcomes, such as healing, protection, prosperity, and divination. The cultural significance of Persian Magic is evident in its influence on literature, art, and folklore, where tales of powerful sorcerers and magical objects abound. The practice often involves communication with spiritual entities, including angels (Fereshteh) and jinn, and requires adherence to specific ethical codes to avoid unintended consequences. The use of symbolism and allegory is prevalent, with dreams and visions considered important sources of knowledge and guidance. Over time, Persian Magic has interacted with other traditions, including Islamic mysticism and Sufism, resulting in syncretic forms of practice that continue to evolve today. The knowledge is often passed down through families or within secretive orders, emphasizing the importance of lineage and initiation. The power of words, both spoken and written, is central to Persian Magic, with specific verses from the Avesta and the Quran used in rituals and incantations. The proper timing of rituals, aligned with astrological events, is also considered crucial for success. The belief in the interconnectedness of all things is a fundamental principle, highlighting the practitioner's role as a mediator between the physical and spiritual realms. It is also used to influence interpersonal relationships, fortune, and control the elements.
subtitle: Ancient arts of influence and power

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Ahura Mazda created the world as a battleground against Angra Mainyu. Magic is a tool used to strengthen the forces of good in this cosmic struggle.

keyMyths.title: The Simurgh's Healing Power
keyMyths.description: The Simurgh, a mythical bird, possesses healing powers and can grant wisdom to those who seek it. Its feathers and presence are often invoked in healing rituals.

sources.author: Mary Boyce
sources.work: Zoroastrians: Their Religious Beliefs and Practices
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of Zoroastrianism, including its magical beliefs.
